title: closure_graph.hexa — DAG navigator over omega_*.json closure corpus (predecessors + realized successors + planned/unrealized followups)
predecessors_note: follow-on from ω-meta-1 (commit a39d14e0 → tool/closure_index.hexa, flat 123-row index). meta-1 gave the corpus a navigable list; meta-2 surfaces the DAG that was implicit in the JSON fields all along.

predecessors.omega_meta_1: shipped tool/closure_index.hexa flat indexer over /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_*.json (123 rows at meta-1 commit, 125 at meta-2 run-time — corpus is live)
predecessors.closure_json_schema_observed_at_meta_2: context.predecessors as DICT in 18 closures, as LIST in 1, top-level predecessors in 9, next_followups all as string-arrays in current corpus (22 strings, 0 objects)

constraints_honored: raw 9 hexa-only-suffix — new file is tool/closure_graph.hexa
raw 80 sentinel — __CLOSURE_GRAPH__ scanned=N nodes=I edges=E orphans=O unrealized=U emitted in every mode

no parallel-collision: no edits to cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*, design/kick/*, .own
closure JSON written here (anima/state, untracked — NOT git add'd in anima)

approach: Single python3 sub-invocation scans the directory and emits a typed line protocol (N/P/F/T/S records) to stdout. The hexa stage1 layer parses these lines, builds parallel-array adjacency maps (predecessors_of, successors_of, raw_preds_of, followups_text_of, unrealized_of), and renders one of six output modes: default text, --json {nodes,edges}, --cycle subgraph, --orphans, --unrealized, or --help. Cycle-name normalization (ω-prefix → omega_, dash → underscore, lowercase) lives in both the python extractor and a small hexa wrapper around python -c so cross-references resolve robustly. Sentinel always emitted last.

deliverables.language: hexa stage1 + embedded python3 for JSON parsing (mirrors closure_index.hexa pattern)

validation_scenarios.name: scenario2_cycle_subgraph
validation_scenarios.actual: ← predecessors: omega_bridge_15, omega_bridge_16, omega_bridge_17 ; → realized_successors: omega_bridge_19
validation_scenarios.note: Spec brief said 'successors include bridge-19/20/21' — corpus reality: only bridge_19 records bridge_18 in its predecessors. bridge_20 has no predecessors recorded; bridge_21 lists 17/19/14_force_add (not 18). Tool faithfully reflects corpus data, not the brief's assumption.
validation_scenarios.result: PASS (chain integrity confirmed: --namespace bridge view shows bridge_15→{16,17,18}, bridge_17→{18,19,21,22}, bridge_19→{21,22})

validation_scenarios.name: scenario3_orphans
validation_scenarios.expected: early Mk.I-floor cycles surface as orphans
validation_scenarios.actual: 92 orphans — dominated by audio-2..7, audio-baseline, network-* baselines, stdlib-* early cycles

validation_scenarios.name: scenario4_unrealized
validation_scenarios.actual: ω-runner-13d (norm=omega_runner_13d, from=omega_runner_13b), ω-runner-13e (norm=omega_runner_13e, from=omega_runner_13c), ω-hexa-batch (norm=omega_hexa_batch, from=omega_runner_13c)
validation_scenarios.result: PASS — exactly the gaps the brief predicted, plus ω-hexa-batch as bonus signal

validation_scenarios.name: scenario5_json_validity
validation_scenarios.command: hexa run tool/closure_graph.hexa --json | sed '/^__CLOSURE_GRAPH__/d' | python3 -m json.tool
validation_scenarios.note: Per closure_index precedent (raw 80), sentinel is always emitted; canonical pattern strips it before JSON parse.

validation_scenarios.name: scenario6_empty_dir
validation_scenarios.actual: EMPTY: no cycles matched (scan_dir=/tmp/empty_for_graph) ; __CLOSURE_GRAPH__ scanned=0 nodes=0 edges=0 orphans=0 unrealized=0 EMPTY

validation_scenarios.name: scenario7_namespace_filter
validation_scenarios.expected: all bridge_* cycles, full chain visible

key_findings: Edge density is sparse (58 edges / 125 nodes ≈ 0.46 edges/node) — most closures are still informational silos. The bridge axis is the densest sub-DAG (almost every node has both preds and succs).

92 / 125 = 73.6% of closures are 'orphans' (no predecessors AND no successors recorded). Most are early Mk.I cycles that predate the context.predecessors convention. This is a metadata-coverage opportunity, not a graph defect.

Only 3 unrealized followups surface across the corpus: ω-runner-13d, ω-runner-13e, ω-hexa-batch — all from the runner-13 chain. This is high signal-to-noise: free-text followups in other closures rarely contain extractable cycle-style references; most are operational nudges (e.g. 'run sweep for 7+ cycles').
Namespace 'bridge' subgraph is the cleanest end-to-end PSC chain in the corpus — 22 nodes wired through the alert-pipeline arc.
Cycle-name normalization is essential: closures use 'ω-bridge-15' / 'omega_bridge_15' / 'omega-bridge-15' interchangeably. Without normalization, no edges would resolve.
next_followups: ω-meta-3 (candidate): annotate orphans — write a tool that proposes context.predecessors entries for orphan closures by analyzing axis text and timestamps (textual nearest-neighbor over the closure corpus).
ω-meta-4 (candidate): closure-DAG-driven CI gate — a closure JSON authored without context.predecessors when the axis namespace already has ≥3 prior closures should warn (raises PSC-loop hygiene).
ω-meta-5 (candidate): generate a Graphviz/D3 HTML view from --json output for human navigation; serve from /tmp for ephemeral inspection.
